summary:
Macrocephaly is a prominent and early sign in Canavan disease, typically appearing between 3-6 months of age, accompanied by hypotonia and confirmed via MRI showing megalencephaly due to accumulated toxic metabolites from deficient aspartoacylase activity. This specific constellation makes Canavan disease uniquely associated with macrocephaly compared to similar disorders lacking significant cranial growth patterns.

The summary above contains only two sentences focusing solely on clinically relevant facts about macrocephaly’s presence and characteristics in Canavan disease without including extraneous details or unrelated information.
